{"description":"Explore workspace structure","prompt":"Explore the workspace at /home/user/workspace to understand the project structure. Look at:
1. What framework/technology is in use (check package.json, next.config, tsconfig, etc.)
2. What the current codebase looks like (pages, components, routes)
3. Any existing admin pages or product-related code
4. Route structure

Be very thorough - list files, read key config files, and examine existing pages/routes.","subagent_type":"Explore"}
attributes.io.output:
[{"type":"text","text":"Here is a thorough summary of the project structure and findings. --- ## Project Overview This is a **Next.js 16** (canary) application using **React 19**, **TypeScript**, and the **App Router**. It was set up from a very minimal initial state ("baseline" commit) with a subsequent "workspace" commit that added the `lib/db.js` file. --- ## Framework / Technology Stack - **Framework**: [Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41](https://nextjs.org/) -- very early canary - **UI Library**: React 19.1.0, React DOM 19.1.0 - **Language**: TypeScript 5.x (strict mode enabled) - **Path aliasing**: `@/*` maps to the project root (`./*`) - **AI dependencies**: `ai` 5.0.59, `@ai-sdk/react` 2.0.0-beta.28, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.17.0, `zod` ^4.0.10 - **Dev/Test**: Vitest ^3.1.3, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `vite-tsconfig-paths` (but no test files exist yet) - **Package manager**: npm (lock file: `package-lock.json`) - **Module system**: ESM (`"type": "module"`) - **No CSS/framework solution** is configured yet (no Tailwind, no CSS modules imports, nothing). --- ## Config Files ### `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` - Minimal -- just an empty `NextConfig` object. ### `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` - Target: ES2017 - JSX: `react-jsx` - Strict mode enabled - Module resolution: `bundler` - Path alias: `@/*` -> `./*` ### `/home/user/workspace/package.json` - Scripts: `dev` (next dev), `build` (next build), `start` (next start) - No ESLint, no Prettier config files exist. --- ## Directory Structure The project is very small, with only **two source directories** outside node_modules: ``` /home/user/workspace/ app/ layout.tsx -- Root layout page.tsx -- Home page (route: /) lib/ db.js -- Simulated database / data layer node_modules/ next.config.ts package.json package-lock.json tsconfig.json .gitignore ``` --- ## Existing Routes / Pages **Absolutely minimal** -- only two files in the `app/` directory: ### `/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx` (Route: `/`) ```tsx export default function Page() { return (

Home

); } ``` A bare-bones home page with just a heading. No styles, no links, no navigation. ### `/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx` A minimal root layout with HTML `` and `` tags wrapping `{children}`. Title/metadata set to "Create Next App" / "Generated by create next app". --- ## Existing Product-Related Code There is exactly **one** product-related file: ### `/home/user/workspace/lib/db.js` A simulated database module that exports a single async function: ```js export async function getAllProducts() { await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100)); return [ { id: 1, name: 'Laptop', price: 999 }, { id: 2, name: 'Phone', price: 699 }, { id: 3, name: 'Tablet', price: 499 }, { id: 4, name: 'Headphones', price: 199 }, { id: 5, name: 'Watch', price: 299 } ]; } ``` This is a **plain `.js` file** (not TypeScript). It simulates a database call with a 100ms delay and returns a hardcoded array of 5 products. Each product has `id`, `name`, and `price` fields. This function is currently **unused** -- no page or component imports or calls it. --- ## Admin Pages **None exist.** There are no admin routes, admin layouts, dashboard pages, or any admin-related code whatsoever. --- ## Route Structure (Summary) | Route | File | Exists? | |-------|------|---------| | `/` | `app/page.tsx` | Yes | | `/admin/*` | None | No | | `/products/*` | None | No | | `/api/*` | None | No | There are no API routes, no dynamic routes, no catch-all routes, and no route groups. --- ## Git History - **2 commits** on the `master` branch: - `f31ff0e` -- "baseline" (initial setup from `create-next-app`) - `026a6b0` -- "workspace" (added `lib/db.js`) - Working tree is clean, …(+749)
